summary: Wall of Sound shines; epic Playing‑In‑The‑Band → Wharf Rat → Truckin' jam
blurb: The set bursts with high‑energy jams, highlighted by a monster Playing > Wharf Rat > Truckin' sequence and a soaring Promised Land opener. Jerry’s guitar soars, Phil’s bass booms, and Keith adds a Chick‑Corea‑flavored swirl. The audience tapes capture the massive Wall of Sound with impressive clarity.
review: July 21, 1974 at the Hollywood Bowl captured one of the Summer ’74 high‑water marks for the Dead. From the opening burst of "The Promised Land" to a second‑set sandwich that rolls from "Seastones*" into a blistering "Playing In The Band → Wharf Rat → Truckin' → Nobody's Fault But Mine → Playing In The Band" suite, the band was locked in and the Wall of Sound roared. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is described as “soaring guitar tone” with brass‑band‑like climaxes and liquid‑country phrasing that lifts each jam. Phil Lesh’s bass bombed through the mix, especially during the 8:50 mark, and his experimental forays on "Seastones*" with Ned Lagin were noted as head‑blowing. Keith Godchaux supplied a Chick‑Corea‑style, Bitches Brew‑ish keyboard texture that cut through the massive PA, while Bill Kreutzmann’s crash cymbals and “Korean” accents added extra excitement to every transition. The audience recordings—particularly the Sony 22p tape chosen as the best source—manage to convey the depth and three‑dimensional stereo spread of the Wall of Sound, delivering clear bass, punchy drums, and only occasional vocal masking. For anyone wanting to hear a quintessential mid‑70s Dead show with epic jams, stellar musicianship, and an unforgettable live atmosphere, this Hollywood Bowl performance is a must‑listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising the same standout jams (Playing‑In‑The‑Band → Wharf Rat → Truckin'), Jerry’s soaring guitar, Phil’s booming bass and experimental Seastones, plus Keith’s vibrant keyboards. No negative or mixed criticism is consistent across sources, so a strong positive adjustment of +1.6 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape that captures the Wall of Sound with good clarity, includes the spectacular second‑set PITB sandwich and minimal technical flaws.

key_highlights: Monster Playing > Wharf Rat > Truckin' jam sequence in the second set
Experimental Seastones collaboration blowing heads off
Wall of Sound captured with vivid three‑dimensional stereo imaging
"The Promised Land" opener sets a high‑energy tone

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring guitar tone with brass‑band end sounds and hot, liquid‑country phrasing that lifts every jam.
band_performance.Phil: Loud, strong bass that "bombs" around the 8:50 mark; experimental Seastones work with Ned Lagin described as head‑blowing.
band_performance.Bob: Beautiful dual‑guitar work on Sugar Magnolia and expressive playing in Spanish‑jam sections.
band_performance.Brent: Keith delivers a Chick‑Corea‑style, Bitches Brew vibe that punches through the mix, solid throughout both sets.
band_performance.Mickey: Bill Kreutzmann’s powerful crash cymbals and splashy Korean‑style accents add extra excitement to the Wall of Sound.
